questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph : 10 moles of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is heated at 15 atm from $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $347^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ assuming volume constant. The pressure at equilibrium is found to be 50 atm . The equilibrium constant for the dissociation of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ $2 \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \quad \Delta \mathrm{H}=91.94 \mathrm{~kJ}$ Can be written as $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}=\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{N}_2} \times\left(\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{H}_2}\right)^3}{\left(\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{NH}_3}\right)^2}(\mathrm{~atm})^2$ Answer, the following questions:Question : The percentage of dissociation of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is
questions.solution:
$\underset{10-2 \mathrm{x}}{2 \mathrm{NH}_3} \rightleftharpoons \underset{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{~N}_2}+\underset{3 \mathrm{x}}{3 \mathrm{H}_2}$

Pressure increases due to increase in temperature as well as due to increase in moles.
$15 \propto 300$
$\mathrm{P} \propto 620$
$\mathrm{P}=\frac{620}{300} \times 15=31 \mathrm{~atm}$ at 10 moles of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ at 620 K .
Now, $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is dissociated to attain 50 atm at 620 K
$\begin{aligned}
& P \alpha \mathrm{n} \text { or } 10 \alpha 31 \\
& (10+2 \mathrm{x}) \alpha 50 \\
& \frac{10+2 \mathrm{x}}{10}=\frac{50}{31}=1.61,10+2 \mathrm{x}=16.12 \\
& 2 \mathrm{x}=6.12 \\
& \alpha=\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{10} \times 100 \\
& \alpha=\frac{6.12 \times 100}{10}=6.12
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
